fact: A group of flamingos is called a “flamboyance.”
Sea otters hold hands while they sleep so they don’t drift apart.
Honey never spoils — archaeologists have found 3,000-year-old honey still perfectly edible.
There are more trees on Earth (about 3 trillion) than stars in the Milky Way.
Norway once knighted a penguin. His name is Sir Nils Olav.
Cows have best friends and get stressed when they’re separated from them.
A single strand of spider silk is stronger than steel of the same width.
Bees can recognize individual human faces.
Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood.
Scotland’s official national animal is the unicorn.
Dolphins give each other names — unique whistles they answer to.
A cloud can weigh more than a million pounds, and still it floats.
The dot over a lowercase “i” or “j” is called a tittle.
Butterflies taste with their feet.
Astronauts can grow up to two inches taller in space.
The world’s oldest known living tree is over 4,800 years old.
Otters keep a favorite rock in a pouch of skin to crack open shells.
A “jiffy” is a real unit of time — one hundredth of a second.
Some sea turtles return to the exact beach where they were born to lay their eggs.
Bananas are botanically berries — but strawberries are not.
The Eiffel Tower can grow more than six inches taller in summer as the metal expands.
Wombats produce cube-shaped poop — the only known animal to do so.
The shortest war in history lasted just 38 minutes.
A baby puffin is called a “puffling.”
